code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test20-APR-2016Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, and ketonuria, with low specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no evidence of infection (negative nitrite and leukocyte esterase). These findings are consistent with possible diabetic nephropathy and/or acute kidney injury. Further clinical correlation and nephrology evaluation are recommended.
